message:
feat(abi): port 11 glibc reserved-namespace signal aliases (bd-ujxkk)

Adds the 11 glibc reserved-namespace aliases for the
signal-management entry points across signal_abi and unistd_abi:

- signal_abi: __sigprocmask, __sigwait, __pause, __raise, __kill,
  __killpg, __sigignore, __sighold, __sigrelse
- unistd_abi: __sigqueue, __sigwaitinfo

glibc emits these as both internal-use entry points and as the
symbols libstdc++ cancellation cleanup, libpthread internals,
and NPTL interruption paths link against. Without them callers
linking statically see undefined-reference errors.

Implementation: thin no_mangle Rust extern "C" wrappers
delegating to the public counterparts — same pattern as the
previously-shipped __pthread_*, __wait* / __dup3 / __pipe2,
__opendir / __closedir, __accept / __bind / __listen /
__sendto / __recvfrom alias families.

Tests: 5 abi integration tests covering: __sigprocmask
SIG_BLOCK + restore round trip; __kill(self, 0) returns 0;
__killpg(pgrp, 0) returns 0; __raise on out-of-range signum
returns -1; __sigignore + __sighold + __sigrelse round trip on
SIGUSR2 with disposition save/restore.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-ule","title":"EPIC: New runtime_math kernels (FDR, SOS, localization, Groebner, approachability, Sobol, proof-carrying policy)","description":"Goal: add new alien-artifact kernels that compile down to tiny deterministic guards/dispatch, without blowing hot-path budgets.

Kernels in scope:
- alpha-investing / sequential FDR controller (controls false escalations under many monitors).
- SOS-derived barrier certificates (offline synthesis; runtime polynomial eval).
- Atiyah-Bott localization fixed-point policy chooser (offline fixed points; runtime selection).
- Groebner-basis normal-form canonicalization of violation signatures (offline basis; runtime reduction table).
- Blackwell approachability controller for multi-objective (latency, risk, coverage) set convergence.
- Sobol low-discrepancy probe scheduler (deterministic coverage acceleration).
- Proof-carrying policy tables (offline synth; runtime table lookup + hash/cert checks).

Non-negotiable:
- Each kernel must specify hot-path cost, update cadence, cached state surface, and a test strategy.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:30:15.520777833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T02:11:54.998226285Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T02:11:38.823974086Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-1v2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-2vf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-2wd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-3ld","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-45d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-abi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-cv9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-gn9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ule","depends_on_id":"bd-l2r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1145,"issue_id":"bd-ule","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Epic Notes: New runtime_math Kernels (How They Serve libc)

### Purpose
runtime_math is the control plane for the membrane: it decides when to stay fast vs go deep, and when to repair vs deny. New kernels are allowed only if they compile to **small deterministic runtime artifacts** (tables, tiny evaluators) with *explicit* overhead.

### Reverse Core Map Anchoring (Surface -> Failure Class -> Math -> Runtime Artifact)
These kernels must tie to concrete libc pressure points:
- **Hot string/memory kernels** -> overlap/alignment/dispatch faults -> localization + proof-carrying tables -> certified dispatch tables
- **Allocator** -> temporal/provenance corruption + contention regimes -> approachability + SOS barrier -> admissibility guard + multi-objective controller
- **Loader/symbol/IFUNC** -> compatibility drift -> Groebner normal forms + localization -> canonical “violation signatures” + resolver table witnesses
- **Conformance monitoring (many tests, many monitors)** -> multiple-testing false escalations -> alpha-investing FDR -> controller that caps false alarms across families
- **Harness probe selection** -> coverage gaps -> Sobol -> deterministic low-discrepancy probe schedules under budget

### Branch-Diversity Reminder (Project Rule)
Major milestones must include at least:
- conformal statistics: already in-tree (`conformal`, `eprocess`) + this epic adds FDR
- algebraic topology: already in-tree (`cohomology`, `persistence`)
- abstract algebra: this epic adds Groebner
- Grothendieck-Serre methods: already in-tree (`higher_topos`) + localization is in the same “geometry/localization” vein

### Runtime Cost Discipline (Non-Negotiable)
For each kernel we require:
- hot-path invocation budget (ns/op) and cadence (per call vs per epoch)
- cached state surface (what lives in `RuntimeKernelSnapshot`)
- deterministic arithmetic (fixed-point if possible; no heavy transcendentals)
- tests that show behavior invariants and that perf gates remain within budget

### Deliverable Shape
Each kernel follows the same pipeline:
1. Design bead: math contract + loss model + artifact format + invariants
2. Implement bead: tiny runtime evaluator/controller (no heavy machinery)
3. Integrate bead: wire into `RuntimeMathKernel::decide/observe` with versioned snapshot fields
4. Tests/perf bead: microbench + property tests + regression thresholds","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:51:50Z"},{"id":1146,"issue_id":"bd-ule","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CobaltCompass: All 9 blockers now CLOSED. Epic complete: alpha-investing FDR, SOS barriers, localization chooser, Groebner normal form, approachability, Sobol, proof-carrying policy tables all integrated with tests and perf validation. 809 membrane tests pass.","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:11:54Z"}]}

Add ABI regression coverage so bad flags stay dominant over null-path faults.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:19:53.600277266Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T22:52:21.734480678Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T22:52:21.732334928Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-xl4sb","title":"PORT: freezero + recallocarray (OpenBSD secret-zeroing allocators)","description":"OpenBSD-origin allocator primitives missing from frankenlibc-abi: freezero(ptr, size) (zero size bytes then free — used for secret-bearing buffers to defeat use-after-free leaks of credentials) and recallocarray(ptr, oldnmemb, nmemb, size) (realloc with overflow check + zero newly-added bytes on grow, and zero released bytes on shrink). Both extend our existing reallocarray to cover the OpenBSD security-hardened allocator API now adopted by glibc 2.34+. Ship as thin shims in malloc_abi.rs delegating to existing realloc/free + memset; no new core module needed since the work is pointer-arithmetic + libc-malloc orchestration.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-25T14:50:29.517540340Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T14:53:57.257027350Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T14:53:57.255131170Z","close_reason":"shipped: freezero + recallocarray in stdlib_abi.rs delegating to malloc_abi+explicit_bzero; 9 integration tests; clippy-clean","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Complements the existing hosts(5) + resolver(5) conformance suite (bd-x7jd).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T21:24:38.654768153Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T21:26:27.989824462Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T21:26:27.987850063Z","close_reason":"Added 12-case services(5) conformance table in commit 73bfe8f.  {"id":"bd-xpgak","title":"xdr_pointer / xdr_reference: no depth limit on caller-supplied recursive procs","description":"## Defect

`xdr_pointer` (rpc_abi.rs:1399) and `xdr_reference` (rpc_abi.rs:1362)
take a caller-supplied per-element xdr_proc. If the caller passes a
proc that recursively invokes xdr_pointer on a nested field (e.g.,
a generated stub for an XDR type with a self-referential
optional pointer like `struct tree { tree *left; tree *right;}`),
attacker-supplied wire data of repeated `more=1` flags walks the
recursion arbitrarily deep — same DoS class as bd-lc5ss but
caller-controlled rather than library-internal.

bd-lc5ss fixed the library's own internal recursive type
(xdr_pmaplist) by rewriting iteratively. That fix doesn't help
caller-supplied recursive types; the library has no per-thread
depth tracker on xdr_pointer/xdr_reference at all.

## Fix

Add a `XDR_RECURSE_DEPTH` thread_local Cell<usize> with a hard cap
(e.g. 256). xdr_reference increments on entry, decrements on exit,
and returns XDR_FALSE if the counter exceeds the cap. This is
defense-in-depth: callers using rpcgen-generated stubs with
sensible types never approach the cap, but a malicious wire stream
fed to a vulnerable caller is bounded by the depth limit instead
of by available stack.

## Severity

[MEDIUM] (P3). The library-internal recursive type was fixed by
bd-lc5ss; this remaining attack surface requires a vulnerable
CALLER (one that passes a recursive proc), so it's a smaller blast
radius than bd-lc5ss but still a real wire-controlled DoS for any
RPC server that exposes recursive XDR types.","notes":"Fixed in 9d326b98: XDR_REFERENCE_DEPTH thread_local Cell<u32> with hard cap of 256. xdr_reference increments on entry, decrements on exit. Added regression test xdr_reference_depth_limit_refuses_deep_recursive_wire_without_overflow with 1024-deep recursive RecursiveNode chain. 90/90 xdr_abi_test pass; clippy clean.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T02:00:51.865514841Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-23T03:53:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T03:53:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-xrmnr","title":"CONFORMANCE: libc math.h core diff matrix","description":"Differential harness shipped in commit 0a96670b. 15 functions (sqrt/fabs/floor/ceil/fmod exact + sin/cos/tan/atan2/exp/log/log2/log10/pow/sinh/cosh/tanh ULP-tolerant), 9 tests. Custom IEEE-754 classification + ULP-distance comparator. Zero divergences at 4-ULP tolerance.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-frankenlibc","created_at":"2026-04-24T08:30:00Z","created_by":"cod-frankenlibc","updated_at":"2026-04-24T08:30:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T08:30:00Z","close_reason":"Harness shipped; 0 divergences against glibc.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","differential"]}

@@ -1688,3 +1688,103 @@ pub unsafe extern "C" fn raise_default_signal(sig: c_int) -> c_int {
 
     error
 }
+
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+// glibc reserved-namespace aliases:
+// __sigprocmask / __sigwait / __pause / __raise / __kill / __killpg /
+// __sigignore / __sighold / __sigrelse
+// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+/// glibc reserved-namespace alias for [`sigprocmask`].
+///
+/// # Safety
+///
+/// Same as [`sigprocmask`].
+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
+pub unsafe extern "C" fn __sigprocmask(
+    how: c_int,
+    set: *const libc::sigset_t,
+    oldset: *mut libc::sigset_t,
+) -> c_int {
+    unsafe { sigprocmask(how, set, oldset) }
+}
+
+/// glibc reserved-namespace alias for [`sigwait`].
+///
+/// # Safety
+///
+/// Same as [`sigwait`].
+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
+pub unsafe extern "C" fn __sigwait(set: *const libc::sigset_t, sig: *mut c_int) -> c_int {
+    unsafe { sigwait(set, sig) }
+}
+
+/// glibc reserved-namespace alias for [`pause`].
+///
+/// # Safety
+///
+/// Same as [`pause`].
+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
+pub unsafe extern "C" fn __pause() -> c_int {
+    unsafe { pause() }
+}
+
+/// glibc reserved-namespace alias for [`raise`].
+///
+/// # Safety
+///
+/// Same as [`raise`].
+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
+pub unsafe extern "C" fn __raise(signum: c_int) -> c_int {
+    unsafe { raise(signum) }
+}
+
+/// glibc reserved-namespace alias for [`kill`].
+///
+/// # Safety
+///
+/// Same as [`kill`].
+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
+pub unsafe extern "C" fn __kill(pid: libc::pid_t, signum: c_int) -> c_int {
+    unsafe { kill(pid, signum) }
+}
+
+/// glibc reserved-namespace alias for [`killpg`].
+///
+/// # Safety
+///
+/// Same as [`killpg`].
+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
+pub unsafe extern "C" fn __killpg(pgrp: libc::pid_t, signum: c_int) -> c_int {
+    unsafe { killpg(pgrp, signum) }
+}
+
+/// glibc reserved-namespace alias for [`sigignore`].
+///
+/// # Safety
+///
+/// Same as [`sigignore`].
+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
+pub unsafe extern "C" fn __sigignore(sig: c_int) -> c_int {
+    unsafe { sigignore(sig) }
+}
+
+/// glibc reserved-namespace alias for [`sighold`].
+///
+/// # Safety
+///
+/// Same as [`sighold`].
+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
+pub unsafe extern "C" fn __sighold(sig: c_int) -> c_int {
+    unsafe { sighold(sig) }
+}
+
+/// glibc reserved-namespace alias for [`sigrelse`].
+///
+/// # Safety
+///
+/// Same as [`sigrelse`].
+#[cfg_attr(not(debug_assertions), unsafe(no_mangle))]
+pub unsafe extern "C" fn __sigrelse(sig: c_int) -> c_int {
+    unsafe { sigrelse(sig) }
+}
